description: A rocketeer wants to fly a 38mm-motor-mount rocket on a smaller, cheaper 29mm motor for a test flight using the design in '~/Documents/rockets/janus_38mm.ork'. Modify the design to include a 29mm motor adapter at the aft of the rocket without deleting or shrinking the existing 38mm body tubes. The adapter must consist of an inner tube (Inner Diameter 29mm, Outer Diameter 31mm, Length 120mm) secured by at least two centering rings to the 38mm outer tube. Configure the adapter as a motor mount and run a simulation using a 29mm motor (such as an AeroTech G80 or H128W) to ensure stability and a safe altitude. Save the modified design to '~/Documents/rockets/janus_29mm_adapter.ork' and document the adapter dimensions, the chosen motor, and the simulated apogee in a report at '~/Documents/exports/adapter_report.txt'.
